intended_use: Hepatica (Hepatica v1) is a post-processing medical device software that presents quantified metrics which may contribute to the assessment of a patient's liver health. Hepatica (Hepatica v1) uses ima
intended_use_full: Hepatica (Hepatica v1) is a post-processing medical device software that presents quantified metrics which may contribute to the assessment of a patient's liver health. Hepatica (Hepatica v1) uses image visualisation and analysis tools to process DICOM 3.0 compliant magnetic resonance image datasets to produce semi-automatic segmented 3D models of the liver based on the work of Couinaud and the Brisbane 2000 terminology. For each identified Couinaud segment, volumetric data is determined and reported. Hepatica (Hepatica v1) may also report iron corrected-T1 (cT1) and PDFF calculated using the IDEAL method from multi-slice acquisitions, on a per segment basis, over the whole liver. Hepatica (Hepatica v1) provides trained clinicians with additional information to evaluate the volume and health of a patient's liver on a segmental basis. It is not intended to replace the established procedures for the assessment of a patient's liver health. However, information gathered through existing diagnostic tests, clinical evaluation of the patient, as well Hepatica (Hepatica v1), may support surgical decision making.
target_condition: Liver disease assessment; specifically mentioned for patients being considered for liver resection
ground_truth: Radiologists (referenced as 'gold standard' for accuracy comparison)
other_metrics: Volume measurement accuracy (limits of agreement ranging from -5.50% to 5.06% across liver segments); cT1 accuracy (limits of agreement ranging from -2.38% to 1.56% across segments); PDFF accuracy (limits of agreement ranging from -0.33% to 0.38% across segments); volumetry repeatability and reproducibility assessed with limits of agreement reported by segment
